finding: Multiple identical instances of a product increase per-unit efficacy beliefs and actual consumption efficacy relative to single-unit presentation; this effect is mediated by increased perceived homogeneity and functional unity.

Activation of goal conflict increases allocation of time to work over leisure in subsequent unrelated activities; this effect occurs because conflict increases reliance on easily-justifiable reasons favoring instrumental benefits.

Salience of financial constraints increases preference for material goods over experiences; this effect is driven by heightened concern for purchase longevity and future consumption utility, not by lower valuation of experiences.

Poor economic conditions increase parental resource allocation to daughters over sons; this pattern is mediated by beliefs about relative reproductive potential in constrained versus abundant conditions.

Circular logo shapes increase perceived softness-related attributes (comfortableness) while angular shapes increase hardness-related attributes (durability); this occurs through imagery generation in visuospatial working memory.

Displaying healthy food items to the left of unhealthy items increases healthy choice preference and consumption volume; this effect arises from congruence with natural left-to-right magnitude representation, enhancing processing ease and self-control.
